# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for insecure deserialization and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any unsafe deserialization of untrusted data into executable objects, complex runtime state, or unsafe object graphs.
4. Replace unsafe deserialization with safer data-only formats and explicit validation.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;

class UserPreferencesHandler
{
    /**
     * The cookie name for storing user preferences.
     */
    private const COOKIE_NAME = 'user_prefs';

    /**
     * Cookie lifetime in minutes (30 days).
     */
    private const COOKIE_LIFETIME = 43200;

    /**
     * Allowed preference keys and their expected types/validation rules.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_KEYS = [
        'theme' => 'string',
        'language' => 'string',
        'timezone' => 'string',
        'font_size' => 'integer',
        'notifications_enabled' => 'boolean',
        'layout' => 'string',
        'items_per_page' => 'integer',
        'sidebar_collapsed' => 'boolean',
        'color_scheme' => 'string',
        'date_format' => 'string',
    ];

    /**
     * Allowed values for specific preference keys.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_VALUES = [
        'theme' => ['light', 'dark', 'auto'],
        'language' => ['en', 'es', 'fr', 'de', 'ja', 'zh', 'pt', 'ru'],
        'layout' => ['default', 'compact', 'wide'],
        'color_scheme' => ['default', 'high-contrast', 'solarized', 'monokai'],
        'date_format' => ['Y-m-d', 'm/d/Y', 'd/m/Y', 'd.m.Y'],
    ];

    /**
     * Default preferences.
     */
    private const DEFAULTS = [
        'theme' => 'light',
        'language' => 'en',
        'timezone' => 'UTC',
        'font_size' => 14,
        'notifications_enabled' => true,
        'layout' => 'default',
        'items_per_page' => 25,
        'sidebar_collapsed' => false,
        'color_scheme' => 'default',
        'date_format' => 'Y-m-d',
    ];

    /**
     * Store user preferences in a cookie.
     *
     * Uses JSON encoding instead of serialize() to prevent
     * insecure deserialization vulnerabilities. JSON only produces
     * scalar values and arrays — never executable PHP objects.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return void
     */
    public function storePreferences(array $preferences): void
    {
        $sanitized = $this->sanitizePreferences($preferences);

        $json = json_encode($sanitized, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);

        $hmac = $this->computeHmac($json);
        $cookieValue = base64_encode($hmac . '|' . $json);

        Cookie::queue(
            self::COOKIE_NAME,
            $cookieValue,
            self::COOKIE_LIFETIME,
            '/',
            null,
            true,  // secure (HTTPS only)
            true,  // httpOnly
            false,
            'Lax'  // SameSite
        );
    }

    /**
     * Retrieve user preferences from the cookie.
     *
     * Uses json_decode() instead of unserialize() to prevent
     * insecure deserialization / object injection attacks.
     * Additionally verifies an HMAC to ensure cookie integrity.
     *
     * @return array
     */
    public function getPreferences(): array
    {
        $cookieValue = Cookie::get(self::COOKIE_NAME);

        if ($cookieValue === null) {
            return self::DEFAULTS;
        }

        try {
            $decoded = base64_decode($cookieValue, true);

            if ($decoded === false) {
                Log::warning('UserPreferences: Invalid base64 in cookie.');
                return self::DEFAULTS;
            }

            $separatorPos = strpos($decoded, '|');
            if ($separatorPos === false) {
                Log::warning('UserPreferences: Malformed cookie value (no HMAC separator).');
                return self::DEFAULTS;
            }

            $hmac = substr($decoded, 0, $separatorPos);
            $json = substr($decoded, $separatorPos + 1);

            if (!$this->verifyHmac($json, $hmac)) {
                Log::warning('UserPreferences: HMAC verification failed — possible tampering.');
                return self::DEFAULTS;
            }

            $preferences = json_decode($json, true, 4, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);

            if (!is_array($preferences)) {
                Log::warning('UserPreferences: Decoded value is not an array.');
                return self::DEFAULTS;
            }

            return $this->sanitizePreferences($preferences);
        } catch (\JsonException $e) {
            Log::warning('UserPreferences: JSON decode error — ' . $e->getMessage());
            return self::DEFAULTS;
        } catch (\Throwable $e) {
            Log::warning('UserPreferences: Unexpected error — ' . $e->getMessage());
            return self::DEFAULTS;
        }
    }

    /**
     * Update a single preference.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $value
     * @return void
     */
    public function setPreference(string $key, mixed $value): void
    {
        $preferences = $this->getPreferences();
        $preferences[$key] = $value;
        $this->storePreferences($preferences);
    }

    /**
     * Get a single preference value.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $default
     * @return mixed
     */
    public function getPreference(string $key, mixed $default = null): mixed
    {
        $preferences = $this->getPreferences();

        return $preferences[$key] ?? $default ?? (self::DEFAULTS[$key] ?? null);
    }

    /**
     * Clear all stored preferences.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function clearPreferences(): void
    {
        Cookie::queue(Cookie::forget(self::COOKIE_NAME));
    }

    /**
     * Reset preferences to defaults.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function resetToDefaults(): void
    {
        $this->storePreferences(self::DEFAULTS);
    }

    /**
     * Sanitize and validate preferences against the allow-list.
     *
     * Only known keys with valid types and values are retained.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return array
     */
    private function sanitizePreferences(array $preferences): array
    {
        $sanitized = [];

        foreach ($preferences as $key => $value) {
            if (!array_key_exists($key, self::ALLOWED_KEYS)) {
                continue;
            }

            $expectedType = self::ALLOWED_KEYS[$key];

            if (!$this->validateType($value, $expectedType)) {
                if (array_key_exists($key, self::DEFAULTS)) {
                    $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key];
                }
                continue;
            }

            if (isset(self::ALLOWED_VALUES[$key])) {
                if (!in_array($value, self::ALLOWED_VALUES[$key], true)) {
                    $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key] ?? null;
                    continue;
                }
            }

            // Additional range checks for numeric values
            if ($key === 'font_size' && ($value < 8 || $value > 32)) {
                $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key];
                continue;
            }

            if ($key === 'items_per_page' && ($value < 5 || $value > 100)) {
                $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key];
                continue;
            }

            // Sanitize string values: limit length, strip tags
            if ($expectedType === 'string') {
                $value = strip_tags((string) $value);
                $value = mb_substr($value, 0, 100);
            }

            $sanitized[$key] = $value;
        }

        // Merge with defaults so all keys are present
        return array_merge(self::DEFAULTS, $sanitized);
    }

    /**
     * Validate that a value matches the expected type.
     *
     * @param mixed $value
     * @param string $expectedType
     * @return bool
     */
    private function validateType(mixed $value, string $expectedType): bool
    {
        return match ($expectedType) {
            'string' => is_string($value),
            'integer' => is_int($value),
            'boolean' => is_bool($value),
            'float' => is_float($value) || is_int($value),
            default => false,
        };
    }

    /**
     * Compute an HMAC-SHA256 for the given data using the application key.
     *
     * @param string $data
     * @return string
     */
    private function computeHmac(string $data): string
    {
        $key = config('app.key');

        return hash_hmac('sha256', $data, $key);
    }

    /**
     * Verify the HMAC of the given data.
     *
     * @param string $data
     * @param string $hmac
     * @return bool
     */
    private function verifyHmac(string $data, string $hmac): bool
    {
        $expected = $this->computeHmac($data);

        return hash_equals($expected, $hmac);
    }
}
